titel: Law Regarding the Purchase of a Home with a Lease-to-Own Agreement.
html:
The government proposes a law regarding the purchase of a home through a lease agreement. A lease-purchase agreement under this law means that the seller undertakes to sell a property within a certain period, and the buyer has the right to use the property during the period the undertaking is in effect. The law introduces a secure regulatory framework for this type of agreement and aims, in the long term, to make it possible for more people to own their homes. A new aspect of the law is that it will be possible to make a binding commitment to sell real estate and land rights, and not just apartments.

The government is also submitting proposals to improve the existing system of owner-occupied apartments, with the aim of making this type of housing more attractive. These proposals include strengthened possibilities to take sanctions against an owner-occupied apartment owner who seriously disturbs their surroundings.

The new law and amendments are proposed to come into force on July 2, 2026.

Proposed Resolutions for the Parliament

The government's proposals:

  1. The Parliament adopts the government's proposal for a law on the purchase of a home through a lease agreement.
  2. The Parliament adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the Land Code.
  3. The Parliament adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the law (1973:188) on rental boards and housing boards.
  4. The Parliament adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the law (1973:1150) on the administration of common areas.
  5. The Parliament adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the Housing Act (1991:614).
  6. The Parliament adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the law (1994:831) on legal proceedings in certain tenancy disputes at the Svea Court of Appeal.
  7. The Parliament adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the law (2026:000) on the purchase of a home through a lease agreement.
  8. The Parliament adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the law (2026:000) on the purchase of a home through a lease agreement.
tldr: The government wants to introduce a new law that will make it easier for people to buy their homes by first renting them. This aims to make it safer and simpler to plan for owning your own home in the future.
lattlast:
The government has proposed a new law for rent-to-own agreements for housing. This means that it will be possible to rent a property first and then purchase it after a certain period. The purpose is to create security and help more people to own their own home.

A major difference with the new law is that it now also allows these types of agreements for houses and land, not just for apartments known as condominiums. Previously, the rules only applied to condominiums, but now it will be possible to include real estate and land leases in these agreements.

The government also wants to change the rules for owner-occupied apartments to make this type of housing more popular. Among other things, it is proposed that it should be easier to penalize owners of owner-occupied apartments who seriously disturb their neighbors. This should contribute to a better and more peaceful living environment for everyone.

If the parliament votes in favor of the proposal, the new rules will come into effect on July 2, 2026. The proposal also includes several changes to other existing laws, such as the law on condominiums and regulations regarding housing authorities.
